title: INC Session 1916 and Khilafat Movement
focus: Highlights the historic Lucknow Pact of 1916 and the city's role as a base for the Khilafat Movement.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 059]
The Historic Congress Session of 1916
A Pivotal Moment
Lucknow witnessed one of the pivotal moments in Indian history: the first meeting of stalwarts Mahatma Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru and Mohd Ali Jinnah during the Indian National Congress session of 1916.
The Lucknow Pact was signed and moderates and extremists came together through the efforts of Annie Besant during this session.
"If the Congress was buried at Surat, it is reborn in Lucknow in the garden of Wajid Ali Shah." — Ambica Charan Majumdar, Congress President

[Page 060]
The Khilafat Movement
Active Base: The Khilafat Movement had an active base of support in Lucknow, creating united opposition to British rule.
Objective: The movement aimed to create a Caliphate and protect the Ottoman Empire's authority.
Unity: It brought together Hindu and Muslim leaders in a common cause against colonial rule.
